dtcs.description: Advanced Diagnostics - DTC U0029 (107): F-CAN Malfunction (BUS-OFF (Motor Control Module (MCM))) (IMA System (Motor)) Advanced Diagnostics - DTC U0029 (108): F-CAN Malfunction (BUS-OFF (Motor Control Module (MCM))) (IMA System (Battery)) The motor control processor and the battery control processor are in the motor control module (MCM). The controller area network (CAN) transmits/receives pulsing signals to/from the control modules simultaneously by using two signal lines (F-CAN H and F-CAN L). When each processor does not receive the signals via the F-CAN lines for a set time, a malfunction is detected and a DTC is stored.
detection_condition: No signals via the F-CAN lines are received for at least 0.5 second.
Diagnosis Details
Conditions for setting the DTC
When a malfunction is detected during the first drive cycle, a Pending DTC is stored in the MCM memory. If the malfunction returns in the next
(second) drive cycle, the MIL comes on and a Confirmed DTC and the freeze data are stored.
Conditions for clearing the DTC
The MIL is cleared if the malfunction does not return in three consecutive trips in which the diagnostic runs. The MIL, the Pending DTC, the Confirmed
DTC, and the freeze data can be cleared with the scan tool Clear command or by disconnecting the battery.

dtcs.description: Advanced Diagnostics - DTC U0029 (107): F-CAN Malfunction (BUS-OFF (Motor Control Module (MCM))) (IMA System (Motor)) Advanced Diagnostics - DTC U0029 (108): F-CAN Malfunction (BUS-OFF (Motor Control Module (MCM))) (IMA System (Battery)) The motor control processor and the battery control processor are in the motor control module (MCM). The controller area network (CAN) transmits/receives pulsing signals to/from the control modules simultaneously by using two signal lines (F-CAN H and F-CAN L). When each processor does not receive the signals via the F-CAN lines for a set time, a malfunction is detected and a DTC is stored. U0029 (107), U0122 (107), U0155 (107), U1205 (120) DTC U0029: F-CAN Malfunction (F-CAN Bus OFF) DTC U0122: Lost Communication With Vehicle Dynamics Control Module DTC U0155: Lost Communication With Gauge Control Module DTC U1205: IMA-CAN Malfunction (PCM-to-Motor Control Module) The integrated motor assist-controller area network (IMA-CAN) uses two signal lines, IMA-CAN H and IMA-CAN L, and it receives and transmits multiple signals simultaneously to/from multiple control modules. When the engine control module (ECM)/powertrain control module (PCM) does not receive the signals via the IMA-CAN lines for a set time, the ECM/PCM detects a malfunction and stores a DTC. The integrated motor assist-controller area network (IMA-CAN) uses two signal lines, IMA-CAN H and IMA-CAN L, and it receives and transmits multiple signals simultaneously to/from multiple control modules. When the powertrain control module (PCM) does not receive the signals via the IMA-CAN lines for a set time, the PCM detects a malfunction and stores a DTC.
detection_condition: No signals via the F-CAN lines are received for at least 0.5 second.
Diagnosis Details
Conditions for setting the DTC
When a malfunction is detected during the first drive cycle, a Pending DTC is stored in the MCM memory. If the malfunction returns in the next
(second) drive cycle, the MIL comes on and a Confirmed DTC and the freeze data are stored.
Conditions for clearing the DTC
The MIL is cleared if the malfunction does not return in three consecutive trips in which the diagnostic runs. The MIL, the Pending DTC, the Confirmed
DTC, and the freeze data can be cleared with the scan tool Clear command or by disconnecting the battery.
CVT System
The ECM/PCM does not receive any signals via the IMA-CAN lines for at least 1 second.
Diagnosis Details
Conditions for setting the DTC
When a malfunction is detected, the MIL comes on and a Pending DTC, a Confirmed DTC, and the freeze data are stored in the ECM/PCM memory.
Conditions for clearing the DTC
The MIL is cleared if the malfunction does not return in three consecutive trips in which the diagnostic runs. The MIL, the Pending DTC, the Confirmed
DTC, and the freeze data can be cleared with the scan tool Clear command or by disconnecting the battery.
CVT System

The PCM does not receive any signals via the IMA-CAN lines for at least 1 second.
Diagnosis Details
Conditions for setting the DTC
When a malfunction is detected, the MIL comes on and a Pending DTC, a Confirmed DTC, and the freeze data are stored in the PCM memory.
Conditions for clearing the DTC
The MIL is cleared if the malfunction does not return in three consecutive trips in which the diagnostic runs. The MIL, the Pending DTC, the Confirmed
DTC, and the freeze data can be cleared with the scan tool Clear command or by disconnecting the battery.

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and then back to ON (II).

Diagnosis Details
Conditions for setting the DTC

(second) drive cycle, the MIL comes on and a Confirmed DTC and the freeze data are stored.
Conditions for clearing the DTC

dtcs.description: Advanced Diagnostics - DTC U0100 (102): F-CAN Malfunction (Powertrain Control Module (PCM) - Motor Control Module (MCM)) (Motor) Advanced Diagnostics - DTC U0100 (103): F-CAN Malfunction (Powertrain Control Module (PCM) - Motor Control Module (MCM)) (Battery) The motor control processor and the battery control processor are in the motor control module (MCM). The controller area network (CAN) transmits/receives pulsing signals to/from the control modules simultaneously by using two signal lines (F-CAN H and F-CAN L). When each processor does not receive the signals from the engine control module (ECM)/powertrain control module (PCM) via the F-CAN lines for a set time, or the received data is abnormal for a specified cycle, a malfunction is detected and a DTC is stored. The controller area network (CAN) transmits/receives pulsing signals to/from the control modules simultaneously by using two signal lines (F-CAN H and F-CAN L). When the information is not sent from the motor control module (MCM) via the F-CAN lines and this condition continues for a specified time or when the information sent from the MCM is abnormal and this condition continues for a specified cycle, the engine control module (ECM)/powertrain control module (PCM) detects a malfunction and stores a DTC. dtcs.description: Advanced Diagnostics - DTC U0155 (106): F-CAN Malfunction (Gauge Control Module - Motor Control Module (MCM)) (IMA System) The controller area network (CAN) transmits/receives pulsing signals to/from the control modules simultaneously by using two signal lines (F-CAN H and F-CAN L). When the battery control processor in the motor control module (MCM) does not receive the signals from the gauge control module via the F-CAN lines for a set time, or the received data is abnormal for a specified cycle, a malfunction is detected and a DTC is stored. U0301 DTC U0301: PGM-FI System Program Version Mismatch The integrated motor assist-controller area network (IMA-CAN) uses two signal lines, IMACAN H and IMACAN L, and it receives and transmits multiple signals simultaneously to/from multiple control modules. When the software version in the engine control module (ECM)/powertrain control module (PCM) and the motor control module (MCM) are mismatched for a set time, the ECM/PCM detects a malfunction and stores a DTC. The integrated motor assist-controller area network (IMA-CAN) uses two signal lines, IMACANH and IMACANL, and it receives and transmits multiple signals simultaneously to/from multiple control modules. When the software version in the FI CPU and the CVT CPU are mismatched (CVT CPU software version is old) for a set time, the powertrain control module (PCM) detects a malfunction and stores a DTC. The motor control processor and the battery control processor softwares which are in the motor control module (MCM) can be updated by software rewriting device connected via the F-CAN lines, When both processors need to be updated but either processor fails rewriting, the indicator comes on and stores a DTC. Each processor stores a program version information, and compare their versions each other by transmitting/receiving via the internal serial communication. If the motor control processor judges that its own software version is old, a malfunction is detected and a DTC is stored. The motor control processor and the battery control processor softwares which are in the motor control module (MCM) and the FI CPU which is in the engine control module (ECM)/powertrain control module (PCM) can be updated by software rewriting device connected via the F-CAN lines. When the battery control processor and the FI CPU, or the motor control processor need to be updated but either processor fails rewriting, the indicator comes on and stores a DTC. Each processor stores a program version information, and compare their versions each other by transmitting/receiving via the serial communication. If the battery control processor judges that its own software version is old, a malfunction is detected and a DTC is stored.

Conditions for setting the DTC

The Pending DTC, the Confirmed DTC, and the freeze data can be cleared with the scan tool Clear command or by disconnecting the battery.
The software version in the ECM/PCM and the MCM are mismatched for at least 1 second.

dtcs.description: Advanced Diagnostics - DTC U1204 (55): IMA-CAN Malfunction (Powertrain Control Module (PCM) - Motor Control Module (MCM)) The integrated motor assist-controller area network (IMA-CAN) transmits/receives pulsing signals to/from the control modules simultaneously by using two signal lines (IMA-CAN H and IMA-CAN L). When the battery control processor in the motor control module (MCM) does not receive the signals from the engine control module (ECM)/powertrain control module (PCM) via the IMA-CAN lines for a set time, or the received data is abnormal for a specified cycle, a malfunction is detected and a DTC is stored. U0029 (107), U0122 (107), U0155 (107), U1205 (120) DTC U0029: F-CAN Malfunction (F-CAN Bus OFF) DTC U0122: Lost Communication With Vehicle Dynamics Control Module DTC U0155: Lost Communication With Gauge Control Module DTC U1205: IMA-CAN Malfunction (PCM-to-Motor Control Module) The integrated motor assist-controller area network (IMA-CAN) uses two signal lines, IMA-CAN H and IMA-CAN L, and it receives and transmits multiple signals simultaneously to/from multiple control modules. When the information is not sent from the motor control module (MCM) via the IMA-CAN lines and this condition continues for a specified time or when the information sent from the MCM is abnormal and this condition continues for a specified cycle, the powertrain control module (PCM) detects a malfunction and stores a DTC. The integrated motor assist-controller area network (IMA-CAN) uses two signal lines, IMA-CAN H and IMA-CAN L, and it receives and transmits multiple signals simultaneously to/from multiple control modules. When the information is not sent from the motor control module (MCM) via the IMA-CAN lines and this condition continues for a specified time or when the information sent from the MCM is abnormal and this condition continues for a specified cycle, the engine control module (ECM)/powertrain control module (PCM) detects a malfunction and stores a DTC. The DC-DC converter uses an internal timer to measure the intervals between serial communication data updates. When an update is not received for a specified time, a SCIMD serial communication reception malfunction is detected and a DTC is stored. The motor control module (MCM) communicates with the DC-DC converter. When the MCM does not receive the signals from the DC-DC converter via the SCIDM serial line, or the received data is abnormal for a specified time, a malfunction is detected and a DTC is stored.
